channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Fender Vintera III Late '60s Precision Bass, Maple Fingerboard, Lake Placid Blue Bass Guitar — $1,306.03
item.description:
$1,306.03 · Brand New · Central Music

The Fender® Vintera III series delivers meticulously crafted vintage recreations that capture the authentic look, feel, and tone of iconic Fender guitars from the '50s, '60s, and '70s.

The Vintera® III Late '60s Precision Bass® features an alder body and maple neck with rosewood fingerboard for classic Fender tone that's full of punch and clarity. The late '60s "C"-shape maple neck with 7.25" radius maple or rosewood fingerboard and vintage-tall frets provides supreme comfort and outstanding feel. At its heart, you'll find a vintage-style late '60s split-coil pickup that delivers all the punchy and powerful, warm and woody tone that made Fender® famous. Other features include a vintage-style 4-saddle bridge for rock-solid intonation and sustain, and vintage-style lollipop tuning machines for enhanced tuning stability.

Embrace the authentic vintage spirit and legendary sound of Vintera® III – where every detail tells the story.

Features

  • Alder Body

item.title: Fender James Jamerson 1962 Precision Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, 3-Tone Sunburst Bass Guitar — $3,121.21
item.description:
$3,121.21 · Brand New · Central Music

The James Jamerson 1962 Precision Bass® – your gateway to the legendary sound that powered the Motown revolution.

James Jamerson is widely recognized as the single most influential bassist of all time. Consistently ranked #1 on “greatest bassists” lists, he laid down the groove on dozens of hit songs that defined the Motown sound. His incredible musical prowess and signature style didn’t just define a genre – Jamerson rewrote the rulebook for what was possible on electric bass, influencing legends like Paul McCartney, Marcus Miller, John Entwistle, and Geddy Lee.

The Bass That Built Motown

Jamerson’s signature instrument was his beloved 1962 Precision Bass – the foundation beneath countless hits that shaped popular music forever. This stunning eracorrect tribute instrument celebrates James’ legacy and his inseparable association with the Precision Bass, delivering the authentic vintage sound and feel that defined an era.

Authentic Vintage Craftsmanship

Every detail honors the original, from the premium Heirloom Nitrocellulose Lacquer finish to the era-correct neck shape that captures the feel of Jamerson’s legendary instrument. The custom James Jamerson ‘62 Precision Bass pickup celebrates those unmistakable tones that anchored the Motown hit factory.

Premium Heritage Features

The Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose™ lacquer finish delivers the authentic spirit and aging characteristics that made the original so special. The premium tone and meticulous attention to detail ensure this bass performs to the highest professional standards. Experience the James Jamerson Precision Bass and feel the difference that inspired generations.
See lessFeatures

  • Alder Body with Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose Lacquer Finish
  • James Jamerson 1962 "C"-shaped Maple neck with Heirloom™ Nitrocellulose Lacquer finish and 7.25" radius rosewood fingerboard
  • Custom-voiced James Jamerson '62 Precision Bass Pickup
  • Vintage-Style Hardware including Pickup and Bridge Covers
  • La Bella 0760M Stainless Steel Flatwound Strings
  • Custom Certificate of Authenticity and Commemorative Booklet

item.title: Fender Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass V with Rosewood Fretboard 2025 - Present Electric Blue — $1,049.99
item.description:
$1,049.99 · Excellent · JJ’s San Francisco Music Gear

Excellent condition Fender Jazz bass. Gently used and haven’t played Much since owning. Setup and restrung with Strings by Aurora. Everything still factory except the strings.

item.title: Fender Vintera III Late '60s Precision Bass, Rosewood Fingerboard, Firemist Gold Bass Guitar — $1,306.03
item.description:
$1,306.03 · Brand New · Central Music

The Fender® Vintera III series delivers meticulously crafted vintage recreations that capture the authentic look, feel, and tone of iconic Fender guitars from the '50s, '60s, and '70s.

The Vintera® III Late '60s Precision Bass® features an alder body and maple neck with rosewood fingerboard for classic Fender tone that's full of punch and clarity. The late '60s "C"-shape maple neck with 7.25" radius maple or rosewood fingerboard and vintage-tall frets provides supreme comfort and outstanding feel. At its heart, you'll find a vintage-style late '60s split-coil pickup that delivers all the punchy and powerful, warm and woody tone that made Fender® famous. Other features include a vintage-style 4-saddle bridge for rock-solid intonation and sustain, and vintage-style lollipop tuning machines for enhanced tuning stability.

Embrace the authentic vintage spirit and legendary sound of Vintera® III – where every detail tells the story.

Features

  • Alder Body

item.title: 1996 Ibanez ATK500 Bass Koa Natural — $789
item.description:
$789 · Excellent · The Music Zoo

This is a 1996 Ibanez ATK500 bass built in Japan. With a natural finish, the bass shows off the koa top quite nicely! There's an ATK-A triple-coil pickup with an active three-band EQ. The bass is in overall very good condition; there's one small dent in the forearm carve and the control cavity cover is cracked. It plays nicely and includes a hardshell case!
  • Solidbody Electric Guitar
  • Koa Top
  • Alder Body
  • Maple Neck 
  • 34" Scale Length
  • 1.65" Nut Width
  • ATK-A Tri Coil Humbucker Pickup
  • 3-Band Active EQ
  • 9 lbs, 6 oz.
  • .840" Neck Thickness at 1st Fret
  • .960" Neck Thickness at 12th Fret

Includes: Hardshell Case

item.title: Ibanez ATK305 5-String Bass Trifade Burst 2007 — $600
item.description:
$600 · Excellent · The Music Zoo

This is a lightly used and in voerall very good to excellent condition Ibanez ATK305 bass built in 2007. Features include an ash body with a bolt-on maple neck, the ATK-A triple-coil humbucker and an active 3-Band EQ. There's light scratching on the pickguard and the battery plate is missing a screw - that's it as far as wear goes. A gigbag is included.
  • Solidbody Electric Guitar
  • Ash Body
  • Maple Neck 
  • 34" Scale Length
  • 1.76" Nut Width
  • ATK-A Tri Coil Humbucker Pickup
  • 3-Band Active EQ
  • 11 lbs, 8 oz.
  • .850" Neck Thickness at 1st Fret
  • .960" Neck Thickness at 12th Fret

Includes Gigbag

item.title: Veillette Mark IV Fretless 5 String Bass (High C), Quilted Maple Top — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Excellent · Let's Make Some Noise! - Bass, Synths, FX and more

Awesome 5 string Fretless (E to C) that has a real organic, stand up like tone. Piezo pickups and Rick Turner electronics (if you know, you know). A one of a kind Veillette as it was built to be able to carry on a plane (shorter headstock). The bass is currently strung with Tapewounds.
Includes a soft case. FB is lightly lined.

item.title: Ibanez SR505AMHL SR Standard 5 String Electric Bass, Mahogany Brown Burst Gloss — $749.99
item.description:
$749.99 · Brand New · Butler Music

The photos shown are actual photos of the bass you will be purchasing.

Weight – 7 lbs 10.7 oz

SR505AMHL – New for 2026

For decades the SR has given bass players a modern alternative. With its continued popularity, Ibanez is constantly endeavoring to answer the wider needs of a variety of players, at a variety of budgets. But no matter what the specs, the heart is the same-SR continues to excite with its smooth, fast neck, lightweight body, and perfectly matched electronics.

****Product Features****

- 5pc Roasted Maple/Walnut neck
The Roasted Maple neck is heat-treated to increase stability, durability, moisture resistance and tolerance of temperature changes.

- Rosewood fretboard
Rosewood fretboard provides a well-balanced solid tone with a focused mid range.

- Medium frets
Medium sized frets enhance the accuracy of your notes.

- Okoume body
The Okoume body provides a bright and solid sound.

- Bartolini® BH2 pickups
The Bartolini® BH2 pickups provide a tight bottom-end, punchy mid-range, and smooth response. The dual-coil style configuration maximizes articulation and a long blade pole piece widens the magnetic field, providing balanced string-to-string pickup response.

- Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ
The Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ is our latest refinement on the concept of simple, straightforward, no-nonsense EQ control. The Boost and Cut for the Mid Control are each centered on slightly different frequencies, an innovation that delivers a preset range of the most favorable bass tones.

- MR5S bridge
The MR5S bridge has adjustable saddles for variable string spacing by +/- 1.5mm, answering every bassist’s specific string spacing needs.

- EQ bypass switch (passive tone control on treble pot)
The EQ bypass control offers precision tonal adjustments, including a switch to choose passive-only operation. When the EQ bypass is activated, the treble tone control knob becomes the global tone control for the passive circuit.

- 3-way Mid frequency switch
The 3-way Mid frequency switch allows players to choose from 3 Mid Frequency options and provides great tonal variety.

item.title: Fender Player II Jazz Bass Rallye Orange — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · Detroit Guitar

The Fender Player II Jazz Bass combines classic Fender design with modern playability, delivering the punch, clarity, and versatility that have made the Jazz Bass a staple for generations of bassists. Built around a resonant alder body and loaded with Player Series Alnico V single-coil pickups, the Player II Jazz Bass offers articulate highs, growling mids, and a tight, focused low end that sits perfectly in any mix.

A Modern "C" maple neck provides a comfortable playing experience, while the 9.5" radius maple fingerboard features rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets for smooth movement across the neck. Whether you're laying down grooves in the studio, performing on stage, or practicing at home, the Player II Jazz Bass delivers the feel, tone, and reliability that today's players demand while staying true to its legendary Fender heritage.

Specs: Alder body; maple neck with Modern "C" profile; satin urethane neck finish with gloss urethane headstock face; maple fingerboard with 9.5" radius; black dot inlays; 20 medium jumbo frets; 34" scale length; 1.5" synthetic bone nut; single-action truss rod with headstock adjustment; 4-saddle standard bridge with 5-screw vintage-style mounting; standard open-gear tuning machines; vintage-style disc string tree; 3-ply parchment pickguard; nickel/chrome hardware. Gloss Rallye Orange polyester finish.

Weight: 8 lbs 12 oz.

Electronics: Dual Player Series Alnico V Single-Coil Jazz Bass pickups. Individual volume controls for neck and bridge pickups with master tone control. JJ pickup configuration. No switching.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1964 - Sunburst (left-handed) — $15,000
item.description:
$15,000 · Very Good · Todd's Gear

From appraiser:

1964-2 Fender Jazz Bass (left-handed) Sunburst, with tortoise pickguard & covers, Serial number L35337.

  • numerous dark body edge finish touch-ups.
  • A bone nut has been installed, otherwise the instrument is stock.
  • The neck is a reconverted slab board dated NOV62A & stamped upside down at the butt joint.
  • All other features date to late 1964.
  • I believe this instrument most likely shipped from Fender as is because it is left-handed.
  • There is wear on the rear upper contour of the bass body as well.
  • The instrument includes the original tug-bar finger-rest.
  • There is a slight stand discoloration on the back of the neck, at the 9th fret.
  • Pots are original and date 304-6426 (26th week of 1964)- all solder joints are original & untouched.
  • There is an additional hole in the pickguard under bass side pickup cover mounting screw.
  • Bridge pickup cover has original foam & the over is mounted on two 5/8" diameter non-stock washers.
  • The upper forearm contour has been "touched up" along with numerous chips on side-edges, with extensive touch-ups around the heel.
  • -Bridge pickup reads: 7.45. thousand Ohms
  • -Neck pickup reads: 8.19. Thousand Ohms DC resistance
  • -The instrument does not have its original hard-shell case, and is housed in a burgundy / maroon gig bag.
  • -This instrument's current replacement value is: $15,500 U.S.D.

item.title: Ibanez BTB605LMBKF 5-String Multi Scale Electric Bass Guitar, Black Flat Finish — $1,199.99
item.description:
$1,199.99 · Brand New · Butler Music

The photos shown are actual photos of the bass you will be purchasing.

Weight – 9 lbs 4.50 oz

BTB605LMBKF  – New for 2026

Without a doubt, your instrument should reflect the dedication you place in your musicianship and creative endeavors. At a certain point, the word ?boutique? comes to mind: Select-grade materials, neck-thru construction, and top quality components, all in a meticulously combined into a stunning pro-level instrument. But what about the upfront investment of such an instrument? That's where Ibanez comes in. Our ability to build small-shop quality, into inspiring yet affordable instruments, is showcased in droves with BTB series basses. - Bass Workshop - For more than 40 years, Ibanez has pioneered new frontiers in bass development, pushing the boundaries of conventional designs to bass players of all styles, affording new avenues of expression and creativity. This spirit of exploration has led to the creation of some of the most successful and popular production basses of all time. Following this same drive to bring new and relevant ideas to the musical forefront is what serves as the basis for this ongoing project, dubbed the Ibanez Bass Workshop. The impetus behind each one of these unique instruments comes from a desire by the Ibanez bass development team to satisfy the particular needs of many players. These inventions may not be for everyone, but for those who yearn to explore new sonic vistas through the medium of bass: The Ibanez Bass Workshop project may give you just what you're looking for.

 Rosewood fretboard
Rosewood fretboard provides a well-balanced solid tone with a focused mid range.

- Medium Stainless Steel frets
The Stainless Steel frets are durable and long-lasting.

- Okoume wing body
The Okoume wing body provides a bright and solid sound.

- T1 pickups
The T1 pickup is a humbucker featuring two separate, individually mounted coils that provides tight bass response, punchy mid-range, and clear high-end. It offers flexible voicing suitable for various genres, and the height of each coil can be set individually for highly nuanced adjustments to the tone. On top of its great sound, the physical design features rounded edges for enhanced ergonomic comfort when using the pickup as a thumb rest. Moreover, the individually mounted coils allow them to be positioned slightly offset from one another. This makes it so the surface of the pickup where the player rests their thumb is directly parallel with the strings, which is not typical with most multi scale basses. This design greatly enhances the comfort and playability of the instrument.   

- Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ
The Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ is our latest refinement on the concept of simple, straightforward, no-nonsense EQ control. The Boost and Cut for the Mid Control are each centered on slightly different frequencies, an innovation that delivers a preset range of the most favorable bass tones.

- MR5S bridge
The MR5S bridge has adjustable saddles for variable string spacing by +/- 1.5mm, answering every bassist’s specific string spacing needs.

- EQ bypass switch (passive tone control on treble pot)
The EQ bypass control offers precision tonal adjustments, including a switch to choose passive-only operation. When the EQ bypass is activated, the treble tone control knob becomes the global tone control for the passive circuit.

- 3-way Mid frequency switch
The 3-way Mid frequency switch allows players to choose from 3 Mid Frequency options and provides great tonal variety.

****Special Features****

- 35-34 inch multi scale
35-34 inch multi scale construction allows the higher strings to be played more easily, while still having adequate tension for lower strings. 

- Neck-through construction
The neck-through construction contributes to massive sustain and smooth access to high fretboard positions.

item.title: Fender Bass Guitar - American Performer Precision - 2025 - Lowest Price on Reverb — $1,098
item.description:
$1,098 · Very Good · Music City Pro Audio

Includes: Bass Guitar and Hard Shell Gator Case

This bass is in perfect functioning and excellent cosmetic condition. SN: US25017305

All items are securely packed to ensure the protection of your investment. We are a preferred seller with stellar feedback and quick shipper status.

item.title: Yamaha TRBX17EW - Translucent Black — $180
item.description:
$180 · Excellent · BANG! Music

Used Yamaha TRBX17EW in Translucent Black finish. PJ pickups. In great shape.

Located in Stafford, VA. Bang! Music has been Stafford's premier music center since 1997. We offer used and new instruments, service, rentals, and consignments. Local pickup or shipped to you.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5 HH Bass w/ HSC – 5-String – Made in USA — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Very Good · The Music Hub Inc.

For sale is an Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5 HH 5-string electric bass, made in the USA. This bass features the classic StingRay design with dual humbuckers, active electronics, and a powerful, punchy tone that works great for rock, funk, worship, studio work, and live performance.

The bass has a beautiful dark translucent green/black finish, maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, chrome hardware, and the Music Man compensated bridge. Serial number visible on the neck plate: H02665.

Features
  • Made in USA
  • 5-string bass
  • Dual humbucker pickup configuration
  • Active electronics
  • 4+1 Music Man headstock
  • Maple neck
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • Chrome hardware
  • Music Man bridge
  • Battery compartment on back
  • Serial #: K02665
Condition

Very clean overall with normal light signs of use. The finish has a glossy look and the hardware appears clean. Neck and headstock look good from the photos. Please see pictures for exact cosmetic condition.

Original hard case included!

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Special 4 H with Roasted Maple Fretboard 2018 - Present - gold — $1,700
item.description:
$1,700 · Very Good · Griffin A.

the Tabs on the battery compartment broke off so you will need something to pry them open (they open very easily) but other than that this bass is in amazing condition. Few tiny and basically unnoticeable dings but that’s it. Currently has a fresh set of flat wound strings on it

item.title: S. Martyn Freedom Headless 34” scale 17.5mm spacing 6 string 7.2lbs - Satin Flamed Maple Burst — $3,990
item.description:
$3,990 · Brand New · Bass and Soul Music Gears

**HAND MADE IN BRAZIL**

Built by one of the most talented and dedicated Luthier in Brazil, with hand picked and exotic Brazilian woods.

*THIS BASS IS LOCATED IN BRAZIL, FREE SHIPPING WORLDWIDE WITH DHL*

**Buyer's responsible for local import tariffs**

If you’re looking for an instrument that blends vintage warmth, modern articulation, and incredible comfort, this S. Martyn Custom Bass delivers exactly that.

Specifications:

* Cedar semi - hollowed body

* 3-piece Marfim (ivory) neck

* Roasted Bolivian Rosewood fingerboard

* 34” scale

* 17.5mm string spacing

* Humbuckers pickups with 3 guitar tappings

*S. Martyn preamp (volume, balance, tone, bass, mid, and treble)

*Switch for mid frequencies: 440hz / 940hz

Tone Profile:

The hollowed cedar body gives this bass a very open, airy, and organic voice with excellent resonance. Expect a warm low end, rich low-mid presence, and a natural acoustic character that responds beautifully to your playing dynamics.

The 3-piece Marfim (ivory) neck adds stability, attack, and note definition, keeping the instrument focused and articulate without sounding overly bright.

The roasted Bolivian rosewood fingerboard contributes warmth, smooth highs, and a complex midrange, adding a refined, broken-in feel right from the first note.

What tones can you achieve?

* Deep, warm vintage tones reminiscent of classic soul, Motown, and jazz recordings.

* Tight and punchy fingerstyle sounds for modern gospel, pop, and R&B.

* Articulate fusion and progressive tones with excellent note separation.

* Smooth fretboard response for melodic soloing and chordal playing.

* Warm, woody fretless like character when playing softly.

* Aggressive growl and midrange punch when digging in with heavier attack.

* Balanced slap tones with a rounded top end that never becomes harsh.

Playing Experience:

The hollowed construction makes this bass exceptionally lightweight and comfortable for long sessions while increasing resonance and sustain.

The 34” scale offers familiar tension and excellent string definition, while the 17.5mm spacing strikes a perfect balance between speed, comfort, fingerstyle precision, and effortless slapping.

This is a bass for players who want an instrument with personality: Organic, responsive, and capable of covering everything from vintage warmth to modern clarity without losing its musicality.


You can check our Instagram @BASSNSOULGEAR for video sample of this bass. If you have any question send us a message.

item.title: NEW Squier Mini Precision Bass - 2-Color Sunburst, In Stock and Ships Fast! The Perfect Bass For You Lil Shredder! — $209.99
item.description:
$209.99 · Brand New · Capital City Guitars

Squier Mini Precision Bass - 2-color Sunburst

Serial Number: ICSC26008777

Honey, They Shrunk the P Bass!

Scaled down for younger players, the Squier Mini Precision Bass delivers the stellar P Bass look and sound in a compact poplar body and a shorter-than-short-scale maple neck. And, like its big brother, the Mini Precision Bass pumps out the familiar P Bass tone through its split single-coil pickup. It’s been said that bass players aren’t born, they’re made. Just kidding, no one’s ever said that. But if your child is a little groove monster in the making, you can’t go wrong with putting a Squier Mini Precision Bass in their hands. For parents, Squier’s Mini guitars and basses, including the Mini Precision Bass, are a go-to choice when selecting an instrument for their aspiring musicians. Start them out right with a great instrument made just for them!

A Precision Bass, only smaller

A rock ’n’ roll icon, the original P Bass design was inspired by the Fender Stratocaster’s contoured double-horn body. The Mini Precision Bass retains the shape but scales it down to nearly three-quarters of its original size, perfect for younger players. Beyond that, it’s a P Bass through and through, decked out with chrome knobs, a rock-solid hardtail bridge, and a vintage-style single-ply pickguard, so there’s no mistaking its heritage.

Timeless split single-coil tone

A workhorse in the studio and on the stage, the P Bass has a legendary tone, owing to its split single-coil pickup design. The Mini Precision Bass pumps out that classic sound — fat low end, punchy mids, and crisp highs. With a single Volume knob and a single Tone knob, the Mini Precision Bass has plenty of tonal variation, whether you want to stand out from the band or tuck in behind the drummer and help hold down the groove.

Comfy neck for small hands

The Mini Precision Bass’s diminutive neck measures in at 28.6 inches, nearly two inches shorter than a typical short-scale bass. And featuring a comfortable C-shape profile and satin urethane finish, it’s incredibly easy to move around on — especially for small hands. Capped with a lively laurel fingerboard decorated with pearloid dot inlays, the Mini Precision Bass’s neck not only looks sharp but also sounds great.

Squier Mini Precision Bass Features:

  • 3/4-size Precision Bass, perfect for beginners and young players
  • Split single-coil pickup produces classic P Bass tone
  • C-shaped maple neck with laurel fingerboard facilitates playing comfort across all 20 frets
  • Durable chrome bridge and sealed die-cast tuners ensure proper tuning stability
  • Synthetic bone nut consistently enriches string resonance

About Capital City Guitars:

We are a small brick and mortar music store, we take pride in earning the respect of our customers. We specialize in safe secure worldwide shipping. Thanks for looking at our items. We always give things a final inspection and going over before shipping. Need anything? Just ask! Contact us directly on our website or call the shop Mon-Sat 11am-6pm PST🎸 😎Thanks,

Capital City Guitars

(360) 956-7097

108 4th Ave. E

Olympia, WA 98501

Please like Us on Facebook & Follow Us on Instagram @capitalcityguitars

item.title: Fender American Ultra Jazz Bass with Maple Fretboard 2019 - 2025 - Cobra Blue — $1,200
item.description:
$1,200 · Very Good · Griffin A.

In near excellent condition other than a few small scratches/dings on The back, and a small paint chip near one of the strap buttons

item.title: Fender MB-98 / MB-SD Mustang Bass Reissue MIJ — $1,325
item.description:
$1,325 · Very Good · Aztec Guitar

2005 Fender Mustang bass CIJ in fiesta red, weight 8lbs. Excellent condition with minor play wear, this bass plays well and sounds great. Comes with a Fender soft case.

item.title: Hofner 185 shortscale bass from 1966 in White Vinyl (Vintage, Restored, Höfner) — $1,100.44
item.description:
$1,100.44 · Very Good · PhilThy Guitars

Up for sale is this beautiful Höfner 185 shortscale bass with the fantastic-sounding Höfner Franz Pix 511 "Nova Sonic" pickups from 1966, finished in the lovely white/black vinyl configuration. Fully refurbished and restored, it's in good condition for its age and delivers that incredibly beautiful, punchy 185 sound that clearly distinguishes itself from its predecessor. This is the last design before the facelift and, amazingly, the first of the new year in 1966.

The 185 is from 1966! Accordingly, it shows signs of wear. There are nicks, dings, scratches, and cracks on the headstock, body, and other parts. Despite its age, the vinyl is in very good condition and shines beautifully after restoration. The vinyl is nicely aligned at the edges and has nearly not shrunk. The binding is intact all around. Due to its age, the vinyl has yellowed slightly to a creamy color. The pickguard also has some scratches and wear, but shines beautifully deep black again after restoration. Both pickups are original and functional, as are all switches and potentiometers. The electronics are original. The neck is straight, leveled, and adjusted.

I gave the 185 a comprehensive overhaul. I thoroughly cleaned, disinfected and polished the paint and vinyl. The mechanics were cleaned, polished and re-lubricated. All the electronics were treated, cleaned, checked and checked for grounding. Every single component was disassembled, checked, cleaned and polished. The neck was adjusted for a good string position. Fingerboard and frets cleaned, treated, oiled and sanded. The 185 is fret-clean and adjusted.

The Bass is ready to roll!

  • 30″ / 76cm Shortscale
  • Pot Dates: 250K 16 (CW1 1966)
  • 2x famous Höfner Franz Pix 511 „Nova Sonic“ staple pickups (Humbucker)
  • Solid double-cutaway body in white/black vinyl
  • black nitro painted maple neck
  • rosewood fretboard, 21 frets
  • Pickup selector switch for each pickup ON/OFF
  • Height adjustable bridge
  • New Höfner roundwound strings (.040 – .100)
  • Weight: 3,3 kg

Information:
It is a vintage instrument. The condition description corresponds to this when you come to me in the shop and would take the instrument directly with you. The temperature gradient when shipping with air freight can lead to changes in the condition of the instrument. e.g. Celluloid pickguard could tear or shrink due to tension. Likewise, pickups due to the wire expansion and shrinkage could lose their function by loosening the solder points. I am very accommodating with complaints, but please note this and do not expect a new instrument.

Please look closely at the pictures and ask as many questions as possible if something is unclear. Prefer a few more questions than to have the surprise afterwards. The shipment is securely packed. You are welcome to take a look at my website for insights, I will show you how I pack and ship by using pictures. There are also other high-resolution pictures here.

item.title: Vintage VJ75MBK Reissued Series 5-String 2010s - Gloss Black — $420
item.description:
$420 · Excellent · MusicGearDeals

Show model guitar. A few light blemishes. Sounds fantastic.


§  Body: Alder

§  Neck: Hard Maple – Bolt On

§  Fingerboard: Maple

§  Scale: 34″/864mm

§  Frets: 20

§  Neck Inlays: Black Block

§  Tuners: Wilkinson® WJBL175

§  Bridge: Adjustable

§  Pickups: 2 Wilkinson® (N) WOJB5F (B) WOJB5R

§  Hardware: Chrome

§  Controls: 2 Volume/ 1 Tone

item.title: Peavey USA Cirrus 5 1995 to Present — $2,000
item.description:
$2,000 · Very Good · Jason's Killer Guitar Gear

Bass guitar is in greart condition! It is missing one knob as shown in picture.

•Five-bolt on neck and body construction

•Walnut neck with wedge fingerboard (graphic reinforced)

•12" radius on 4 string; with 24 fret and 15" radius on 5 with 24 frets 15" radius on 5 and 17" radius 6 string

•35" scale length; 24 frets

•Tilt headstock

•Hipshot reduced mass “mini” bass machine heads

•Two internally active VFL™ pickups

•On-board active 3-band EQ with bass/variable/mid/treble boost and cut (10 dB)

•Low impedance output (18 volt powered)

•All satin gold finished hardware

•Fully shielded electronics

•Individual fingerstyle bridge system (string thru or deop slot with (Patented Dual Compression Tech) (three-way adjustable)

•Graphtech™ topnut

Construction:

- Body

The body of this instrument is offered in two versions of the finest woods available in a three piece grain matched Mahogany or a Walnut with Bubinga Top. The deep double cutaway design provides unimpelded access to the highest fret while the extended upper horn balances the instrument, thereby avoiding the need to support the instrument with the left hand. The Cirrus features our hand-rubbed oil/ultra satin finished.

- Neck

The neck is available in Walnut with a wedge fingerboard (Graphite Reinforced) both 4 and 5 string models are equipped with a two away adjustable steel Torsion Rod. A 12" radius; 24" fret and 34" scale. Fingerboard on 4 string model, 15" radius; 24 fret and 35" scale fingerboard on 5 string model.

- Pickups

Each Cirrus instrument comes equipped with two internally active VFL™ pickups. The low impedance output increases the amount of signal-to-noise and produces a brilliantly strong signal over a wide frequency spectrum. The sensitivity level of each pickup may be adjusted by raising or lowering the pickup with height adjustment screws. Raising the pickup closer to the strings will increase sensitivity and lowering the pickup (away from the strings) will decrease the sensitivity.

- Bridge System

The bridge system of this instrument is satin gold finished individual fingerstyle (string thru or drop slot) three way adjustable, with (Patented Dual Compression Tech). The individual saddle tracks adjust to determine both string height and intonation. Solidly anchored to the body (The Patented Dual Compression System) provided maximum punch, sustain, and string stability during the heaviest of playing.

- Battery

The internal preamp places the Cirrus above other bass guitars of its type. It does, however, require two 9-volt batteries to do this. They are accessible by removing the battery cover plate from the back of the instrument.

Controls:

- Volume

The volume knob (1) controls the total signal level from the magnetic pickups and the preamp to the output jack. Refer to Millenium

- Tone Network

The tone network utilized is of the active 3-band EQ type. Each control [Low (2), Mid (3) and High (4)] offers 10 dB of boost (clockwise - CW) or cut (counterclockwise - CCW).

- Blend

The blend control (5) is offered for a wider variety of sounds than available from conventional pickup selector switches. It may be varied from the neck pickup (full clockwise - CW) or any blend in-between.

- Output Jack

The output jack is a genuine Switchcraft®* 1/4" phone jack which accepts standard guitar cables. (We recommend high-quality Peavey shielded instrument cables). * Switchcraft is a registered trademark of Switchcraft, Inc.

item.title: Moollon P Classic IV — $4,300
item.description:
$4,300 · Excellent · Kurt's Bass Gear Emporium

If you know, you know. Moollon basses are what Fender would have made if they'd never cut corners — hand-built by Young Joon Park in Korea, with obsessive Pre-CBS spec hardware including his own custom-made tuners, 20 ultra-thin layers of nitrocellulose lacquer, quartersawn maple neck, Macassar ebony board, and a hand-wound pickup based on YJ's personal 1959–60 Fender Precisions. There is nothing else quite like it.

This one has never left my house and has been out of the case a handful of times. It's newer than any instrument hanging on display at a music store.

Here's the story: I ordered it in late 2020. It arrived in 2025. By then, every band I was playing with that could use a 4-string had disbanded, and everything I'm doing now calls for a modern active 5. This bass deserves to be played by someone who will actually play it.

This is your shortcut. Direct from Moollon, the wait is 4+ years. This one is ready to ship today.

item.title: Epiphone El Capitan J-200 Studio Bass Left-handed USE - Vintage Sunburst — $553.40
item.description:
$553.40 · Excellent · Centre de Musique Victor Music Store

Basse Epiphone J-200 Acoustique El Capitan Gauchère Usagée - Vintage Sunburst


- Boîte d'origine incluse

item.title: Squier Sonic Precision Bass Midnight Blue — $269.99
item.description:
$269.99 · Brand New · Island Music Company

The Squier Sonic Precision Bass is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed, offering iconic Fender style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. This P Bass sports a slim and inviting "C"-shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5" nut width and a thin, lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier split single-coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone. Further details of this model include a 4-saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment, open-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning and durable chrome-plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

Body Material: Poplar
Inlays: Dot
Number of Frets: 20
Scale Length: 34 inches
Nut Width: 1.5 inches
Neck Material: Maple
Fretboard Material: Indian Laurel
Finish/Color: Midnight Blue
Middle Pickup: Ceramic Split Single-Coil
Case: None

item.title: Limited Run Squier Classic Vibe '60s Precision Bass with Matching Headstock 2021 - Sonic Blue — $350
item.description:
$350 · Excellent · Jared’s music sales

This was a fender special run with matching headstock.

Excellent used condition - mark here or there but overall great condition. no major scratches or dents - one small ding I couldn’t get to come out in photos.

item.title: Fender Player Precision Bass with Pau Ferro Fretboard 2018 - 2024 - Black — $675
item.description:
$675 · Excellent · Quentin's Gear

For sake this Fender Precision Bass Made in Mexico. I’m the first owner, upgraded the bridge to a Badass Bass II. I will send the original fender bridge in the case along with fender manuals.

Case Fender included.

I just don’t play bass anymore so I do not need it anymore.

Feel free to ask any questions!

item.title: Squier MB-4 Modern Bass 2005 - Skull and Crossbones — $325
item.description:
$325 · Very Good · M T's Gear Outlet

This 2005 Squier MB-4 Bass with skull emblem, is mis-matched with an Ibanez hard shell case. The guitar has some small nicks and scratches that do not affect play. The case shows signs of road wear with its own scratches and nicks but is completely functional, as is the guitar. This was my brother’s guitar and I don’t know why it has a mis-matched case.

item.title: Vintage Fender Precision 1973 - Three Tone Burst - LIGHT 8.6 lbs absolutely stunning my #1 — $3,995.95
item.description:
$3,995.95 · Excellent · Chechunka’s Shop

I am battling cancer.  This has been my #1 for the last 4 years.  It is my favorite bass.  I do not want to sell it.  It is perfect. It plays amazing.  It sounds amazing.  When I got the bass the body was completely stripped.  I had the amazing guys at Seuf Guitars refinish it (in nitro of course) in what appeared to me the original three tone burst.  They did an amazing job and tastefully relic’d to taste.  A previous owner did a refret at some point and they did a great job.  You can tell the planed the fretboard and everything.  It’s killer.  Seuf was impressed.

The metal and wood are all original to my knowledge. The pickup I replaced with a Fralin and the harness I used a classy boutique harness (I believe it has a treble bleed circuit.  I’ll pop the guard to confirm.  The pickguard is a relic period correct piece from “fenderparts” on eBay.  Sadly looks like they’re no longer making stuff.  They did outstanding work. 


This bass is stunning and considering the new non custom shop James Jamerson p bass is $3k I feel like this is a steal for what you’re getting. An absolute stone cold #1 gun killer. If you're in the market for my forever p bass, this is it.

Weight 8.6 lbs.

Neck: I would call it a nice medium C. Not too big, not to slim. Juuuuuuust right.

Pickup:  Fralin standard wound

Case:  recent fender TSA style

Strings:  DA NYXL 45-105 round wound

Feel free to message me with any questions.  I am selling a dream Les Paul gold top as well (7.6 lbs).  Hopefully it will sell first and I can keep this.

I will include photos of it pre refin so you can have for your records.

All sales are final.  No warrant my implied.  I MAY consider trades for another p bass plus cash on your end.

item.title: Original 1953 Fender Precision Bass *Tadeo Gomez* — $9,500
item.description:
$9,500 · Very Good · Tidewater Vintage

For your consideration Tidewater Vintage offers this incredible 1953 Fender P-Bass built by the one-and-only Tadeo Gomez from start to finish.

As seen, this guitar body was stripped of its original finish back in 1980 but overall it looks great, all electronics, pots and pickup are original, the pickup was rewound by Lindy Fralin. The bridge and pickguard was replaced, original pickgaurd is included. No extra holes have been added that we see.

Condition is Very Good showing normal players wear, scratches and chips but overall presents great and plays even better!

All electronics, tuners, truss and controls work as they should

Please message with any questions and we will reply promptly

Also included is the 1960s Fender Bass case that is in good condition as well.

item.title: 1982 Daion Power Mark XX-B Bass W/OHSC MIJ — $1,275
item.description:
$1,275 · Very Good · Tidewater Vintage

1982 Daion Power Markk XX-B Bass        
SN: 821000
Condition: Very Good showing light players wear, small blemishes mainly above the tailpiece (as seen) otherwise, a great looking bass
Color: Walnut
Mod's/Upgrades: Knobs replaced
Breaks Or Repairs: No
Weight: 8lbs 2oz
Scale Length: 34”
Nut Width: 1 11/16”
Case: Original Hardshell Case

The Daion Power Mark XX-B is a vintage, high-end electric bass guitar produced in Japan by Yamaki between approximately 1978 and 1984. Known for its "neck-through" construction and premium materials, it was the flagship model and top-of-the-line in Daion’s Power Series.

Specifications

  • Construction: Features a 9-piece laminated through-body neck made of alternating maple and rosewood.
  • Body: Multi-piece body typically made of ash with maple or rosewood laminations.
  • Pickups: Equipped with a "P/J" pickup configuration, consisting of a split-coil at the neck and an 8-pole single coil at the bridge.
  • Fretboard: 24-fret (two-octave) rosewood fingerboard featuring dot markers and ivory-colored celluloid binding.
  • Hardware: Heavy-duty brass nut and bridge saddles for improved sustain.
  • Controls: Dual volume and tone knobs with a three-way pickup selector switch

item.title: Squier Sonic Bronco Bass - Laurel Fingerboard, White Pickguard, Daphne Blue — $259.99
item.description:
$259.99 · Brand New · Alto Music

The Squier Sonic® Bronco® Bass is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed, offering iconic Fender® style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. This Bronco Bass sports a slim and inviting "C"-shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5" nut width and a thin, lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier® single-coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone. Further details of this model include a short 30" scale length, 4-saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment, sealed-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning and durable chrome-plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

item.title: Fender AVRI '62 Jazz Bass 2006 in 3-Color Sunburst — $1,599.76
item.description:
$1,599.76 · Fair · Mainly Basses and Bass Gear, Mainly.

Original specs are pasted in below, it looks like this bass has had a modification to the standard volume/volume/tone setup which is not out of style with a bass of this era, a popular modification and it works with this one. Not carried out by myself but it all works great and the bass plays very well, having a typical and reassuring jazz bass sound.

I've tried to highlight the imperfections and damaged areas, nothing other than cosmetics which will bother some people, others (me) not bothered.

Contact me for more information or individual pictures. I didn't bother taking it to bits as it's a relatively modern instrument, if you'd like to see the innards, I can do this for you on request.

All the accessories are in the case including mutes and the template required to drill the holes for the pickup covers and to mount the mutes. The brass earthing strip as also included for the user to fit if required.

Can post, can meet up and exchange or you can collect.

Product Specs. (From the Fender website)

BodyAlder

Body FinishNitrocellulose Lacquer

Body ShapeJazz Bass®

Bridge4-Saddle American Vintage Bass

Bridge Cover/TailpieceIncludes Chrome Bridge Cover

Bridge PickupAmerican Vintage Single-Coil Jazz Bass®

Color3-Color Sunburst

ConfigurationSSControl KnobsBlack Plastic

ControlsStacked Concentric Controls Volume 1. (Neck Pickup), Tone 1. (Neck Pickup), Volume 2. (Bridge Pickup), Tone 2. (Bridge Pickup)

Dimensions6.75x17.00x50.50 IN

FingerboardRosewood

Fingerboard Radius7.25" (184.1 mm)

Fret SizeVintage-Style

Hardware FinishNickel/Chrome

Model NameAmerican Vintage '62 Jazz Bass® (1999-2012), Rosewood Fingerboard, 3-Color Sunburst

Neck FinishGloss Nitrocellulose Lacquer

Neck MaterialMaple

Neck PickupAmerican Vintage Single-Coil Jazz Bass®

Neck Shape"C" Shape

Number of Frets20

Nut MaterialSynthetic Bone

Nut Width1.5" (38.1 mm)

OrientationRight-Hand

Pickguard4-Ply Brown Shell

Pickup ConfigurationSS

Pickup SwitchingNone

Position InlaysWhite Dot

Refinement Neck MaterialMaple

Refinement Neck ShapeC Shape

Scale Length34" (864 mm)

SeriesAmerican Vintage

String NutSynthetic Bone

Tuning MachinesAmerican Vintage Reverse Open-Gear Bass

item.title: Warwick Rockbass Corvette Basic 4 2010s - Ocean Blue Transparent — $400
item.description:
$400 · Very Good · cody's Gear Garage

Great bass. Barely played!

item.title: Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass Fretless 2020 - Present - 3-Color Sunburst — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Excellent · Windmill Guitars TN

Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass – Excellent Condition w/ Fender hard case

Smooth, punchy, and incredibly versatile, this American Professional II Jazz Bass delivers the classic Fender tone that has defined countless recordings and stages.

This bass is in excellent condition, plays effortlessly, and comes complete with the original hard case. Whether you’re laying down vintage grooves or modern lines, it’s ready for anything you throw at it.

Professionally inspected, sounds fantastic, and is ready for its next gig.

Message us for pricing or stop by Windmill Guitars in Cleveland, TN.

item.title: Squier Sonic Precision Bass - Laurel Fingerboard, White Pickguard, Midnight Blue — $269.99
item.description:
$269.99 · Brand New · Alto Music

The Squier Sonic® Precision Bass® is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed, offering iconic Fender® style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. This P Bass® sports a slim and inviting "C"-shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5" nut width and a thin, lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier® split single-coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone. Further details of this model include a 4-saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment, open-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning and durable chrome-plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Special 4 H with Ebony Fretboard- Candyman — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Excellent · Dries Guitars

Absolutely mint

Barely played

Case kept

All candy and case included

Thumb rest is screwed through one of the pickguard holes. NO ADDITIONAL HOLES DRILLED FOR THUMBREST.

item.title: Squier Mini Precision Bass, 2-Color Sunburst — $209.99
item.description:
$209.99 · Brand New · Hilton Music Center, Inc.

The Squier Mini Precision Bass® is the perfect companion for players seeking a reduced-size instrument with classic styling and familiar Fender® tone. Highlights include a thin and lightweight body, a short-scale neck with an easy-to-play "C"-shaped profile, a Squier split single-coil pickup with volume and tone controls for sonic variety, and a hardtail bridge for solid tuning stability.

item.title: Squier Sonic Bronco Bass, Daphne Blue — $259.99
item.description:
$259.99 · Brand New · Hilton Music Center, Inc.

The Squier Sonic® Bronco® Bass is ready to launch any musical adventure into warp speed, offering iconic Fender® style and inspiring tone for players at any stage. This Bronco Bass sports a slim and inviting "C"-shaped neck profile with a narrow 1.5" nut width and a thin, lightweight body for optimal playing comfort while a Squier® single-coil pickup delivers punchy bass tone. Further details of this model include a short 30" scale length, 4-saddle hardtail bridge for precise intonation adjustment, sealed-gear tuning machines for smooth, accurate tuning and durable chrome-plated hardware that is sure to catch the spotlight.

item.title: Fender Vintera '60s Jazz Bass with Pau Ferro Fretboard 2019 - 2023 - Firemist Gold with Seymour Duncan Quarter Pounds — $600
item.description:
$600 · Excellent · Huy's Gear Closet

This is a catch-and-release for me! I thought I wanted to learn how to play bass alongside the guitar, but it didn’t take me very long to find out that I’m just not a rhythm guy. This 2019 Vintera Jazz Bass is in excellent condition and the original pickups have been replaced with Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound (SJB-3) pickups for higher output and improved fidelity.

item.description:
$480 · Very Good · Yuval's Boutique

Amazing little acoustic-electric bass guitar. Comes with premium gig bag.

item.title: Guild Westerly Collection B-240EF Fretless 2010s - Natural — $350
item.description:
$350 · Excellent · Becket

This is a Guild Acoustic.Electric fretless bass. I bought it about 6 years ago and have used it maybe 2 or 3 times. It is in excellent condition and has the original strings on it. It has a warm, rich, deep bass end and nice clear tone. It uses a 9-volt battery and platys really well and easily. It comes with a Gator gig bag.

item.title: Carvin SB5000 Tuners 2012 - Black — $95
item.description:
$95 · Excellent · Terminus Audio

Carvin SB5000 Tuners for 5-String Model

Black

Includes all mounting hardware and grommits

item.title: Fodera Monarch Custom Bolt-On 5 string 2015 - Satin — $7,499
item.description:
$7,499 · Excellent · Riley's Bass Space

This bass is a Fodera Monarch custom bolt-on (not a standard, or standard special) 5 string in absolutely mint condition! If you are familiar with Fodera you know the standard basses are built on a batch basis (30 to 40) in one build run. Custom basses are built on a one at a time basis making them more expensive to produce. For the Aguilar DCB pickups, Fodera did not wire a split-coil option on this bass.

The two switches are:
1. Active / Passive
2. Mid Frequency Select (changes the center frequency for the mid EQ)

The pictures used are a combination of the original Fodera gallery pictures and the bass as it is now. Vinny and Joey signed it for me when it was in for a check up.

Neck has been PLEK'd by Fodera.

Model: Monarch bolt-on

Number of strings: 5

Bass: solid body

Neck Construction: bolt-on

Scale Length: 34 inches

Fret Count: 24

Body Wood: ash

Top Wood: buckeye burl

Neck Wood: 3 piece maple hard rock

Fingerboard Wood: Indian Rosewood

Finish: acrylic satin

Finish Color: clear

Electronics: Fodera/Pope custom 3 band, Monarch

Pickups: Aguilar DCB

String Spacing: 19mm

Fingerboard Inlay: mother of pearl dots

Weight: 9 pounds

Comes with original case (no longer available), which is also in mint condition

item.title: 2010s NS Design CR5 Radius Fretless — $2,499
item.description:
$2,499 · Excellent · Telluride Music Co.

By the time he started NS Design in 1990, Ned Steinberger’s headless guitar designs had already captured the industry’s attention. With NS Design, each model achieves a new level of functionality, providing modern players with the ultimate freedom of expression. The CR5 Radius bass reflects that philosophy in full—a sleek, forward-thinking 5-string built to expand what a bass can do while maintaining exceptional ergonomics, balance, and playability. 

The CR5 Radius start's with the classic NS headless neck, and although its double-cutaway shape ins't exactly out of this world, in your hands it's unlike any other bass on the planet. Built with a flame Maple Top, the CR5 body is shaped around the Radius concept—the graceful curve of the top is matched on the back, introducing a comfort against your body like no other bass. Under the hood, custom magnetic EMG pickups pair with the NS Polar bridge-mounted piezo system, all driven by an 18-volt onboard preamp for additional headroom and tonal flexibility. 

This CR5 Radius was made some time in the 2010s, and it features the Fretless fingerboard option with NS Design's gorgeous Charcoal Satin finish. It shows some minor play wear and signs of use, particularly on bass edge of the body where your arm rests across it and the treble side by the input jack. The back has some scuffs, and there's a ding on the end of the neck worth mentioning (see photos). The pervious owner preferred roundwound strings, lend a nice feel but also punch and dynamics. But they do tend to mar up the fingerboard for than flat wounds.

As if the 5-String neck didn't add enough versatility. the fretless platform opens the instrument up considerably. Singing sustain, expressive slides, and vocal-like character make this bass equally well suited to jazz, fusion, ambient work, and modern session playing. With active electronics, you can dial in the perfect sound. Throughout, the low B remains focused and articulate, while the headless design contributes to exceptional balance across the instrument. This 2010s NS Design CR5 Radius Fretless has a lot to offer! Original gig bag included.

item.title: 2016 Fender American Standard Jazz Bass V — $1,200
item.description:
$1,200 · Good · Nick

I have a 2016 Fender J Bass V here that doesn’t get the attention it deserves. If you want a nice playing Fender but don’t want to give them your money to sue PRS with, here’s your chance!


It’s been gigged, and there are a couple paint chips and dings (see pictures).


All hardware is stock. Comes with a locking Fender composite case (two keys). I’ll leave the strap on it if you want.

item.title: Fender Mustang Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 1978 Sunburst — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Very Good · ATL Bass

This is a great example of a Mustang bass from 1978. Ash body, maple neck, Rosewood board and a beautiful 3 tone burst finish that this era is known for. The knobs are original and period correct for 1978. The sound is thick and full with a good fundamental. It is strung with fresh flatwound strings that thump & are perfect for roots music. Add round wounds and it’s ready for rock. It weighs over 9 lbs and that really comes through in the tone. Despite its size, this is a substantial bass. Ships in nice gig bag. Shipping to the USA is included in the price. There are various scratches and dings on the body and a finish ding on the treble side of the neck near the headstock (see pictures).

item.title: 1973 Rickenbacker 4001 Bass Mapleglo w/original Toaster pickup FLAME MAPLE NECK! — $4,795
item.description:
$4,795 · Very Good · Olivia's Vintage

Olivia's Vintage would like to present this 1973 Rickenbacker 4001 in its original Mapleglo finish. It has a great playing flame maple neck/center piece with great frets. It's all original and 100% complete with the exception of the hand rest. Another pickguard has been on the bass at one time as there are additional screw holes concealed underneath the pickguard. A later road case is included. This bass is in great condition with some minor nicks + dings, light belt buckle wear and other minor cosmetic wear. The bass itself remains structurally sound with no cracks, breaks or repairs. All electronics work and sound great as it provides an authentic early '70's 4001 bass tone with its original "toaster" pickup in the neck position.
We are happy to ship internationally.

item.title: Fender JB75-90US 1993/4 - Natural — $1,345.79
item.description:
$1,345.79 · Excellent · Daves Guitars

Jazz Bass , Made in Japan, serial number N080173 , Fuji-gen plant ,1993/4 , 4.6kg , including hard case, excellent condition, no chips or dings .
